Design Brief — A320 Fire Panel 45VU

Artist Reference & Recreation Specification
Panel 45VU — Overhead Fire Detection & Extinguishing Aircraft Airbus A320 Family FCOM Ref DSC-26-20-10 / DSC-26-20-20 Issued 2026-05-31 Source key photo = FCOM photo   model = Blender render   fcom = FCOM text   est = engineering estimate

Reference Images

A — FCOM Reference Photo Real aircraft overhead panel. Authoritative source for all light colors, label positions, guard geometry, and proportions.
B — Current Fire Panel (Best Blender Version) Orthographic front render of eng1_left_panel_BEST.blend. Use as geometry base — improve materials and realism to match Ref A.
C — Hydraulic Panel (Style Benchmark) Best-version render of the HYD panel. Shows the finish quality, flow-art lines, Futura labels, and FAULT/OFF cell treatment to match or exceed on the fire panel.
Objective: Match Reference A (FCOM photo) in material realism and finish. Use Reference B as the geometry starting point. Use Reference C as the quality bar — the fire panel must reach or exceed this level of Blender craftsmanship.

Light States

CellColorTrigger — ONTrigger — OFFSource
FIRE pb face RED Fire detected (loop A or B) Fire extinguished / reset fcom L44423
SQUIB cell WHITE Flight crew releases (pulls) the FIRE pb AGENT pb pressed fcom L44434
DISCH cell AMBER Squib of the agent fires (bottle discharged) — fcom L44436
All cells (normal ops) OFF / DARK — No fire condition fcom

AGENT Pushbutton (× 2 per engine — AGENT 1 and AGENT 2)

PropertyValueSource
Body size14 mm × 14 mm (square)model
LayoutAGENT 1 left of FIRE pb — AGENT 2 right of FIRE pbphoto
Cell splitSQUIB top (55%) / DISCH bottom (45%)photo model
SQUIB triggerWhite — when flight crew releases (pulls) FIRE pbfcom L44434
DISCH triggerAmber — when squib of agent fires (bottle discharged)fcom L44436
Inner frameThin light-grey inset border on DISCH cell only (#7A7A7A)model
LabelsAGENT — top; 1 / 2 — small sub-label; Futura Medium whitephoto

Workflow Phases

Phase 1

Vector Recreation

  • Trace panel outline from Ref A (FCOM photo)
  • Map all pb positions and sizes to scale
  • Typography: Futura Medium for all labels
  • Produce clean vector master (.ai / .svg) with layers
  • Overlay on Ref A at 1:1 — verify alignment
Phase 2

3D Modelling in Blender

  • Import Ref B (.blend) as geometry base
  • Correct any proportion discrepancies vs Ref A
  • Add panel wear, edge bevels, surface micro-detail
  • Replace placeholder materials with full PBR shaders
  • Rig guard hinge — open and closed poses
  • Backlit emission materials for all light states
  • Quality target: match or exceed Ref C (HYD panel)
Phase 3

Mechanical Design Review

  • Front ortho render vs Ref A — side-by-side
  • Verify SQUIB / DISCH cell proportions and positions
  • Verify guard alignment and pivot point
  • Review all 4 light states: OFF / RED / WHITE / AMBER
  • Confirm screw head count and positions
Phase 4

FBX / GLTF Export

  • Export as .fbx and .glb
  • Bake PBR: albedo, roughness, metallic, normal, emission
  • Separate material slots per light state
  • Pack all textures into export folder
  • Verify import into three.js viewer
Phase 5

Assumptions Sheet

  • List every dimension estimated from photo
  • List materials not visible in Ref A
  • Flag geometry not present in Ref B
  • Note any FCOM vs photo conflicts
  • Submit with Phase 3 review package
Phase 6

Deliverables Package

  • Source .blend (self-contained, fonts packed)
  • FBX + GLB exports
  • Texture maps (PNG ≥ 2048 × 2048)
  • Front renders — all 4 light states
  • Assumptions sheet (PDF or Markdown)

Modelling Standards

  • Scale — 1 Blender unit = 1 mm. Panel is 300 × 105 mm.
  • Geometry origin — centre of panel face at Z = 0.
  • Topology — SubD-ready. No N-gons on curved surfaces. Bevels on all hard edges ≥ 0.1 mm.
  • Materials — PBR Principled BSDF only. Bake to image textures for export. Emission nodes for backlit cells.
  • Font — Futura Medium from /System/Library/Fonts/Supplemental/Futura.ttc, packed into .blend with font.pack(). Self-contained — no external font reference.
  • Light states — Separate material slots: OFF, FIRE RED, SQUIB WHITE, DISCH AMBER. Render layers or material override per state.
  • Guard — Separate object. Pivot at top edge centre. Two poses: closed (flush) and open (≈ 90°).
  • Naming — FIRE_PB_ENG1, AGENT_PB_1_ENG1, GUARD_ENG1, PANEL_45VU, etc.
  • Self-containment — All textures and fonts packed. File opens correctly on any machine.
  • Review renders — HDRI neutral studio lighting. Front orthographic at 1200 × 600 px.

Assumptions Sheet Template

Complete and submit with Phase 3 review. List every value that could not be directly measured from Ref A or Ref B.

Example
FIRE pb recess depth: estimated 3 mm
No depth visible in front-face photo. Based on standard A320 pb form factor. Confirm with physical measurement.
Guard hinge gap
Estimated 0.5 mm clearance between guard bottom edge and panel surface
Not measurable from Ref A. Ref B (Blender model) shows ≈ 0.5 mm. Marked as estimated.
Backlight diffuser
Assumed frosted acrylic diffuser behind pb face legends
Standard A320 pb construction. Not visible in Ref A. Inferred from lit-state glow in photo.

Deliverables Summary

Vector master
.ai or .svg with named layers — panel, pbs, guards, labels, cells
Blender source
.blend — self-contained, fonts packed, all materials named
3D exports
.fbx + .glb with baked PBR textures (albedo, rough, metallic, normal, emission)
Review renders
Ortho front — 4 states: all OFF, FIRE RED, SQUIB WHITE, DISCH AMBER
Texture maps
PNG ≥ 2048 × 2048 per material. Separate emission map per light state.
Assumptions sheet
PDF or Markdown. Every estimated value listed with reasoning.
